purpose: Route large mutable caches, AI artifacts, generated runtimes, process evidence, and agent scratch data away from the limited Btrfs system root and into {{ABYSS_MACHINE_SRV}}.
principle: Keep stable configs, manifests, service units, policy, hook contracts, and compact state on /. Put large or fast-growing generated data directly on {{ABYSS_MACHINE_SRV}}. Store host facts and low-level snapshots under {{ABYSS_MACHINE_STATE}}. Do not leave compatibility symlinks unless the operator explicitly approves them.

system_root.role: OS, packages, small configs, service definitions, compact state
avoid_for: container images when migration downtime is available

large_root.role: large mutable host-owned caches, AI artifacts, generated runtimes, and future agent scratch roots

protected_roots.reason: Abyss OS project material. Host storage automation may read but must not write or clean here.
Stack repository/model material under reformation; host layer must not mutate it.
Stack/service material; host layer must not mutate it.
User source repository; host layer must not mutate it.
Operator-owned game library. Never a machine cleanup/write target.
Operator-owned game library. Never a machine cleanup/write target.
Freelance/work projects. Machine-owned data must not be routed here.
Work path. Machine-owned data must not be routed here.

commands.storage_write_preflight: abyss-machine storage write-preflight --kind KIND --bytes BYTES --target PATH --json

direct_paths.reason: host AI compile caches, TTS artifacts, Hugging Face runtime cache, and benchmark caches are large and regenerable
host-managed AI Python runtimes are large and wrappers point here directly
Intel NPU compile cache can grow quickly
model and tokenizer downloads should not consume the system root
package wheels are large and regenerable
browser automation binaries are large and regenerable
browser automation binaries are large and regenerable
OpenVINO probe blobs can be multi-gigabyte
host-owned temporary AI/model/runtime work should use the large data mount instead of persistent /tmp for large artifacts
local-private nervous-system artifacts such as screenshots and browser page-content captures must not consume the limited system root
local-private browser DOM text captures are bounded calibration evidence and must not use the system root

hook_points.ordering: lexical within /etc stage dir, then lexical within /srv stage dir
hook_points.enforcement: non-zero hook exit blocks only when abyss-machine storage run-hooks is called with --enforce

process_snapshots.rule: Use process snapshots for low-level context before/after heavy storage, runtime, AI, or container investigations; do not treat a snapshot as exclusive workload attribution.

categories.active_agent_state: agent state; keep unless operator reviews manually
categories.host_runtime: host runtimes/toolchains; keep current, review obsolete experiments manually
categories.active_ai_artifacts: current AI artifacts; cleanup needs model/profile awareness
categories.project_source: source trees; no machine cleanup from this layer
categories.rebuildable_cache: useful cache; can be regenerated when storage pressure matters
categories.redownloadable_heavy: can be downloaded again but has network/offline/time cost
categories.cleanup_candidate: generated temporary data; cleanup by age after checking active processes
categories.stale_archive_candidate: likely old archive; operator review before move/delete
categories.migrate_not_delete: move to better storage route; do not delete
categories.operator_review: mixed or personal data; no automatic action

classification.rule: Inventory and recommendations are facts-only. They never delete; cleanup requires explicit operator action and usually a pre_cache_cleanup hook.

pressure.rule: Pressure classifies / and /srv and ranks pressure valves. It is facts-only and does not authorize deletion.

cleanup_plan.rule: Cleanup-plan runs process guard by default, emits operator steps, and never deletes. A future apply command must re-run guard and hooks immediately before one explicit action.

monitor.load_policy: light inventory only, no full inventory, process guard interval 0 by default, no deletion
monitor.rule: This is the recurring read-only storage route. Future agents should read monitor/latest first, then pressure/cleanup-plan details if needed.

write_preflight.command: abyss-machine storage write-preflight --kind KIND --bytes BYTES --target PATH --json
write_preflight.protected_roots_rule: {{ABYSS_OS_ROOT}}, /srv/GAMES, /srv/games, /srv/work, /work, abyss-stack roots, and unknown /srv/* are denied for machine-owned writes
write_preflight.rule: Run before large downloads, model caches, runtimes, benchmark artifacts, container storage, temp data, or generated artifacts.

apply.rule: Apply consumes one cleanup-plan action, re-runs monitor/process guard, runs hooks for actual confirmed actions, and only executes allowlisted action types.

completed_migrations.reason: Rootless Podman graphroot is routed directly to {{ABYSS_MACHINE_SRV}}/storage. Running containers and named volumes are still work-sensitive, but this is no longer a root-space migration candidate.
completed_migrations.method: direct rootless Podman storage.conf graphroot; no symlink tail

work_context.rule: Read /srv/work project rules read-only before stopping work-related containers or reviewing Podman volumes; do not mutate work projects from host storage automation.

agent_rules: Before downloading or generating large assets, read this policy and prefer {{ABYSS_MACHINE_SRV}}/cache or {{ABYSS_MACHINE_SRV}}/storage.
Before any large machine-owned write, run abyss-machine storage write-preflight --kind KIND --bytes BYTES --target PATH --json.
Do not place model downloads, benchmark artifacts, compiled OpenVINO blobs, NPU blobs, or browser automation binaries directly on / unless a stable package manager requires it.
Do not write to or clean {{ABYSS_OS_ROOT}}, /srv/GAMES, /srv/games, /srv/work, /work, abyss-stack roots, or unknown /srv/* from host storage automation.
Do not leave compatibility symlinks for machine-owned caches or runtimes; update configs and wrappers to direct {{ABYSS_MACHINE_SRV}} paths.
Do not use /work for machine-owned caches, runtimes, model artifacts, logs, process snapshots, or host bridge state.
Before large writes or runtime creation, run or honor the matching abyss-machine storage hook stage; store evidence through abyss-machine JSON commands.
Use abyss-machine processes snapshot --json for low-level process state when diagnosing storage pressure, background load, AI runtimes, or container activity.
Rootless Podman graphroot is already routed to {{ABYSS_MACHINE_SRV}}/storage; verify with abyss-machine storage podman-preflight --json before any volume or container-storage work.
Before stopping work-related containers or touching Podman volumes, read detected /srv/work AGENTS/README/compose files read-only and preserve named volumes and bind mounts.
Use abyss-machine storage inventory --json for lightweight recurring classification and abyss-machine storage inventory --full --json for manual deep review.
Use abyss-machine storage pressure --json before large writes and when free space changes; use abyss-machine storage cleanup-plan --json before any cleanup.
Use abyss-machine storage monitor --json, or {{ABYSS_MACHINE_STATE}}/storage/monitor/latest.json, as the first recurring freshness point for storage state.
Before executing cleanup, run abyss-machine storage apply --action-id ID --dry-run --json first; actual apply requires --confirm and still blocks on active processes/protected paths.
Treat rebuildable and redownloadable caches as pressure valves, not garbage: delete them only when storage pressure is real or the operator explicitly chooses slower future rebuild/download.
If cleanup-plan reports blocked_active_process, do not touch that path until the process exits or the operator explicitly accepts the runtime impact.
Do not mutate abyss-stack repositories or model roots from this host storage layer.
